title: Domain Event
description: Describes a domain event in an event-driven scalable architecture, following CloudEvents conventions. Domain events enable loose coupling between microservices through async message passing over Kafka, RabbitMQ, or cloud message brokers.

id.description: Unique identifier for this event occurrence.

source.description: URI identifying the context in which the event occurred (typically the originating service).

type.description: Event type in reverse-DNS notation describing the kind of occurrence.

subject.description: Identifies the subject of the event in the context of the source (e.g., entity ID).

datacontenttype.description: Content type of the event data.

dataschema.description: URI identifying the schema for the event data payload.

time.description: Timestamp of when the event occurred in RFC3339 format.

data.description: Domain event payload specific to the event type.

partitionkey.description: Key used to route the event to a specific Kafka partition for ordering guarantees.

correlationid.description: Correlation ID for tracing a business transaction across multiple services.

causationid.description: ID of the command or event that caused this event (event sourcing lineage).

metadata.description: Additional metadata properties for routing, filtering, and tracing.

retryCount.description: Number of times this event has been retried.

deadLetterQueue.description: Whether this event has been moved to the dead letter queue.
